description_long: This is a small, rustic cabin composed of two separate structures, each with a gable roof and visible wooden slat siding. The larger structure features a covered porch area with railings and an overhanging roof, while the smaller structure appears to be a detached outbuilding or shed. Both buildings have small, rectangular windows distributed asymmetrically along the sides, and the main cabin has a prominent front entrance beneath the porch. The roofs are steeply pitched and extend slightly beyond the walls, providing additional shelter. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian yet cozy design, suitable for residential or recreational use in an urban or park setting.
description: A rustic, two-part wooden cabin with gable roofs, a covered porch, and asymmetrical window placement. The main structure is accompanied by a smaller outbuilding.
description_view_0: Side view showing both the main cabin and the smaller outbuilding, highlighting the porch and window arrangement.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the gable roofs and the covered porch of the main cabin, with the outbuilding directly behind.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the rear and side windows of both structures, with minimal architectural detail visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the main cabin and outbuilding, showing the gable rooflines and limited window placement.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch and slight overhangs, constructed from wood or similar material
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple small rectangular windows arranged asymmetrically along the sides of both structures, with no clear pattern or clustering
attribute_door_placement: single main door centered beneath the porch on the front facade of the main cabin; no doors visible on the outbuilding
